engineering.translation: The yoga-yoked one, of pure self (viśuddha-ātmā), self-conquered (vijita-ātmā), with senses subdued (jita-indriyaḥ), the self-as-self-of-all-beings (sarva-bhūta-ātma-bhūta-ātmā) — though acting (kurvann api), is not stained (na lipyate). The verse closes the equivalence-and-preference block by describing the karma-yogi at full operational form. Five marks: pure self, self-conquered, sense-controlled, identifying with the self-of-all-beings, acting without being stained. The engineering analog is the senior who has internalised the equivalence-and-preference and now operates from it. They ship; they are not the ship. They review; they are not the review. They identify with the team and the system as a whole, not with the individual artifact. The passage coheres with 4.20 and 2.47-2.48; it is the operating form of action-without-bondage.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A staff engineer chairs the architecture review for the Postgres-vector-DB decision. She runs the meeting tightly, defends three architectural commitments, gives ground on two others, asks the new hire from the data-platform team for input and treats the input on its merits. She files the GitHub decision-doc; she sets up the Datadog dashboards for the migration; she lands the first migration PR. Through the four-hour meeting and the subsequent two weeks of execution she is fully engaged — hands on the keyboard, name on the doc, voice in the room. And: at no point during the engagement does she identify herself with the decision being made. If the architecture-review group rejects her primary commitment, she will hear the rejection, document the decision, and execute the alternative. She is not stained by the decision because she is not the decision; she is acting on it. The five marks are operationally readable: viśuddha, vijita, jita-indriyaḥ, sarva-bhūta-ātma-bhūta-ātmā, na lipyate.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader hears 'not stained' as 'doesn't care'. The senior in the example cares about the decision more than most — she has prepared for the meeting for weeks. What the verse refuses is the identity-collapse — the engineer who *becomes* the decision and is wounded by its rejection. Care is preserved; identity-collapse is not.
engineering.counter_example: When the engineer's distance from the work has become true detachment — when the work is not actually being done, when the engagement is performative rather than real — the verse does not endorse this. The kurvann api ('though acting') in the verse is structurally significant; the staining-free state is *while acting*, not *while withdrawing*.
engineering.implication: Distinguish, in your last hard call, between care for the decision and identification with it. The senior the verse describes carries the first all the way through; the second is what they have released.
engineering.quotable_line: She is fully engaged. She is not the engagement. The verse calls this not-stained-while-acting.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 5.7 engineering layer drafted from triangulated source pack. Anchored to chapter-5 thesis: equivalence-of-paths-with-karma-yoga-preference, decoupled identity, sama-darśana, internal-bliss-source, brahma-nirvāṇa-as-operational-rung-with-honest-STRETCHED. Cross-references to chapters 2-4 preserved.
